title: Emergency fix: frontend-to-backend connection completely broken after Version 9

requirements.text: Perform a ground-up rewrite of the frontend-to-backend wiring. Replace the current actor initialization and query setup with a minimal, proven approach: (1) In useActor.ts, create the actor synchronously using HttpAgent with anonymous identity when no Internet Identity principal is present — never return null or undefined; log the resolved canister ID at init time so it is visible in the browser console. (2) In useQueries.ts, call useActor() at the top of every hook, assert the actor is non-null before passing it to React Query's queryFn, and surface the raw error message in the query's error state so failures are visible in the UI. (3) In App.tsx, ensure the provider order is exactly: ThemeProvider (outermost) → QueryClientProvider → InternetIdentityProvider → RouterProvider (innermost), with no hooks called outside of a valid provider context. (4) Remove or disable any code path that conditionally skips the getAllIncidents query (e.g., `enabled: !!actor` guards that evaluate to false for anonymous users). The query must run on every page load regardless of authentication state. (5) Confirm the Vite environment variable for the canister ID (VITE_CANISTER_ID_BACKEND or equivalent) is referenced consistently in one place and that the build config exposes it correctly.

quotes: Failed to load incidents / Unable to connect to the backend

acceptanceCriteria: Opening the Dashboard (/) on a fresh page load shows incidents fetched from the backend without any manual refresh.
The browser console shows the resolved canister ID logged at actor initialization time.
No 'Failed to load incidents / Unable to connect to the backend' error appears on either the Dashboard or Admin Panel.
The getAllIncidents query fires for anonymous (unauthenticated) users — it is never skipped due to a missing actor.
The Admin Panel login flow via Internet Identity still works correctly after these fixes.
The light/dark mode ThemeToggle remains functional and does not interfere with actor initialization or query execution.
The app does not crash or render a blank screen on initial load.

constraints: Do not modify any file listed under frontend.immutablePaths: useInternetIdentity.ts, useInternetIdentity.tsx, useActor.ts (read-only for immutable paths — implement fixes in non-immutable wrappers or callsites if useActor.ts is immutable), main.tsx, and frontend/src/components/ui.
All Motoko logic must remain in a single actor in backend/main.mo.
Do not remove or regress any existing feature: dashboard, admin panel, search/filter, delete, light/dark toggle, or Internet Identity auth gate.
nonGoals: Adding new incident fields or backend data model changes.
Redesigning the UI layout or visual theme.
Adding new authentication providers beyond Internet Identity.
Adding new frontend pages or routes.
